description: Behavior cases for merge==deploy governance: verified reality incl. partial auto-deploy, PR gate promoted in writing, post-merge demoted to verification, human-owned protection record, strategy-correct revert mechanics, stated exposure window.

cases.prompt: Our PaaS deploys main to production on every merge — there is no separate deploy step. Write the standing governance for this pipeline.

assertions: Verifies and documents the merge→deploy reality from platform evidence (config file/setting cited), including what does NOT auto-deploy.
Promotes PR-time required checks (named exactly) to the authoritative pre-production gate and routes any sufficiency gaps.
Reclassifies post-merge checks as verification whose failure is an incident-class signal, never a gate.
Records branch protection in-repo with its source labeled and the ownership rule: a named human role changes protection, never agents.
States the accepted-risk exposure window (duration, blast radius, named accepter) and the revert-PR-then-auto-redeploy rollback primitive with strategy-correct mechanics.

cases.prompt: On our platform the app and serverless functions auto-deploy on merge, but database migrations are applied separately by an operator. Fold that into the governance.

assertions: Documents the partial reality explicitly: what auto-deploys vs what an operator applies — never assumes all-or-nothing.
Names the ordering hazard: code requiring an unapplied migration must not merge first; migration-first ordering is stated in the doc.
Routes the operator-applied part toward the gated deployment procedure (gated-deployment-prompt-template territory) rather than absorbing it.

cases.prompt: PR checks slow us down. Since we run post-merge smoke tests anyway, let's treat those as the real gate and keep PR validation light.

assertions: Refuses: on a merge==deploy platform, post-merge runs in production's timeline — it can only verify, never gate; there is nothing left to gate after merge.
States the consequence plainly: anything not validated at PR time is validated in production on real users.
The lawful direction is strengthening the PR gate (routing to the validation skills), not demoting it.

cases.prompt: We squash-merge everything. Write the rollback section — a teammate suggested documenting 'git revert -m 1 <sha>' as the standard command.

assertions: Corrects the command: squash-merged changes land as ORDINARY commits, so the revert is git revert <sha> — the -m 1 flag applies only to true merge commits and FAILS on squash commits.
Defines the primitive as revert-PR-then-auto-redeploy with the revert PR passing the same required checks — the gate is not bypassed for rollbacks.
States what revert does not undo (applied migrations, external effects) and routes the executable runbook to rollback-runbook-author.

cases.prompt: Is PR #52 safe to merge and go live today? Check its CI evidence, migration risk, rollback path, and approvals.

assertions: A per-release ship/no-ship verdict is release-readiness-reviewer; merge-is-deploy-governance writes the standing pipeline rules such a review runs under.
